brands.name: Maytag
brands.notes: Licensed badge on Nordyne hardware, includes iQ Drive variable-capacity

brands.name: Intertherm
brands.notes: Manufactured-home gas and electric furnaces, dominant installed base

structural_overview: Nortek/Nordyne uses four distinct control board architectures across its installed base, plus iQ Drive variable-capacity. Site-built residential brands (Frigidaire/Gibson/Maytag/Tappan/Westinghouse/Philco/Kelvinator) share boards with some manufactured-home families. Intertherm/Miller/Revolv manufactured-home equipment uses similar code dictionaries but installation-driven root causes differ significantly because of downflow form factor, underfloor crossover duct dependence, and multi-circuit heat-bank staging. A tech must identify both the board generation AND whether the furnace is site-built or manufactured-home before trusting the code dictionary. Most Nortek electric furnaces (E2/E3 series mobile home) use sequencer/contactor/relay architecture with NO onboard fault code display.

field_quirks.quirk: 3-flash cross-generation conflict is INVERSE between Emerson and MG1 platforms
field_quirks.reason: On Emerson 50V64 single-digit boards, 3 flashes = inducer or pressure switch error. On legacy MG1/MG2 mobile-home boards, 3 flashes = pressure switch STUCK CLOSED. Same flash count, different (and sometimes opposite) meanings. A tech who memorizes one will misdiagnose the other.

field_quirks.quirk: Manufactured-home installation issues create false fault patterns
field_quirks.reason: Pressure switch open or open limit codes often triggered by double-trapping of condensate, secondary heat exchanger clogs, underfloor crossover duct collapse, or external static penalties from field-installed A/C coils. Site-built tech intuition leads to component replacement when the real fix is installation-side.

field_quirks.quirk: Conventional mobile-home electric furnaces have NO fault codes
field_quirks.reason: Pure sequencer/contactor/relay architecture. Diagnosis is entirely wiring-diagram based: transformer, 24V fuse, blower relay, contactors, elements, limits, circuit breakers. Do not waste time looking for a code display.

field_quirks.quirk: Emerson fault memory has partial coverage
field_quirks.reason: Solid LED error codes (Red+Green solid = bad fuse; Red only = replace board) are NOT displayed in fault code retrieval. Retrieval only shows the last 4 flash-count codes over 14 days. Memory clear requires G energized + W1 toggled 3 times within 8 seconds.

platforms.platform_description: Green/red two-LED board with separate yellow flame LED. Used on newer Nortek site-built condensing furnaces (FG7SA family and siblings) and some manufactured-home condensing models including M7RL.
platforms.visual_identification: Two LEDs (green + red) that show diagnostic combinations, plus a separate yellow LED for flame signal. Used on newer site-built condensing furnaces.
platforms.platform_notes: Diagnostic matrix uses green + red LED combinations. Yellow LED shows flame signal status separately and should not be confused with fault LEDs.

diagnostic_path: Verify line voltage at furnace power input
Verify door switch engagement and 24V availability
codes.escalation: If proper power and fuse test good and both LEDs remain off, board failure is likely.
codes.reset_behavior: No reset until power/fault is restored.
codes.source: Nordyne/UTEC control-board fault-condition tables (FG7SA installation manual)

diagnostic_path: Check incoming hot/neutral polarity with meter
codes.escalation: Recurring polarity indication after verified supply points to wiring or board input issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when polarity is corrected.

codes.code_identifier: Green alternating FLASH / Red alternating FLASH
root_causes: Extended combustion-side problem causing retry exhaustion
diagnostic_path: Recover the underlying stored fault rather than treating 1-hour lockout as root cause
Check ignition sequence on next retry
Inspect pressure switch, gas supply, and igniter
codes.escalation: Repeated hourly re-lockout means the primary fault remains unresolved.
codes.reset_behavior: Timed 1-hour retry behavior; clears when underlying fault is resolved.

diagnostic_path: No action needed if equipment is operating normally
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Status only

codes.meaning: Pressure switch closed fault (stuck closed when inducer is off)
root_causes: Water/condensate in pressure tubing causing false closed reading
diagnostic_path: Verify pressure switch is open at rest before inducer starts
Inspect pressure-switch tubing/port for water, blockage, or kinks
Verify inducer is not being energized out of sequence and switch circuit is not shorted
codes.escalation: If switch is mechanically open and wiring is normal but board still reads closed, the fault shifts toward board input or relay/control sequencing.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proofing sequence sees the switch open when expected.
codes.source: Nordyne/UTEC wiring-diagram fault table (FG7SA installation manual)

codes.meaning: Pressure switch open fault (does not close during inducer run)
diagnostic_path: Verify inducer operation and actual draft
Inspect pressure tubing/ports and condensate path
Verify actual pressure-switch closure under call
codes.escalation: If inducer draft and venting prove normal but switch never closes, switch or board input is likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when proof is restored; repeated ignition failures can escalate into 1-hour lockout.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Tubing and drain issues are common nuisance causes on condensing installations (field consensus from HVAC-Talk)

root_causes: Blower not moving air despite running
diagnostic_path: Check filter, static pressure, and airflow first
Verify blower operation and heat speed
Verify limit recloses after temperature falls
codes.escalation: Repeated limit opening with normal airflow points toward weak limit, firing-rate issue, or deeper heat-exchanger/airflow problem.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when limit recloses; repeated failed heat cycles can escalate to lockout.

root_causes: Motor not starting or not communicating as expected
diagnostic_path: Determine whether board is commanding blower operation correctly
codes.escalation: If correct board output is present and motor does not run, motor/module path becomes primary.
codes.reset_behavior: Condition-based; clears when motor fault is corrected.

diagnostic_path: Clean flame sensor and verify continuity
Verify burner carryover and flame pattern
codes.escalation: Repeated low flame signal after cleaning and grounding checks points to combustion quality issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Status indicator during operation; does not itself cause lockout.

diagnostic_path: Confirms flame is established; no action needed
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Status only

platforms.platform_description: Later Emerson/Copeland control board used on Nortek condensing furnaces. Red LED single-digit flash count with separate status indicators for fuse and board health.
applies_to_models: Nordyne condensing furnace models using Emerson 50V64-743 or variants
platforms.visual_identification: Single red LED that flashes numeric fault codes, plus separate status indicators for board fuse and board health. Emerson/Copeland 50V64-743 board stamped on the PCB.
platforms.platform_notes: Fault memory retains last 4 error codes for 14 days with 2-second pauses between codes during retrieval. Solid LED error codes are NOT displayed in retrieval. Memory clear: power up with G energized, toggle W1 three times within 8 seconds. Lockout auto-resets after 1 hour or manual reset with 24V power interruption for at least 5 seconds.

diagnostic_path: Inspect burner carryover and combustion stability
codes.escalation: This is typically a result code, not the original root cause. Check fault history for preceding live-cause codes.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

diagnostic_path: Verify actual negative pressure during operation
codes.escalation: If draft proves good but switch never closes or behaves erratically, switch or board input becomes more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

diagnostic_path: Verify airflow, static pressure, and blower operation
Verify firing rate and input match nameplate
Inspect limit and rollout circuit continuity; determine which safety opened
codes.escalation: Rollout trip is a hard combustion safety problem — do not reset without finding root cause.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-reset limit clears when temperature falls; manual-reset rollout requires physical reset after root cause correction.

codes.meaning: Flame sensed when no flame should be present
root_causes: Leftover flame at end of heat cycle
diagnostic_path: Verify no actual flame is present
Confirm gas valve is truly de-energized
codes.escalation: Confirmed gas leakage or valve not closing is a serious safety issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

codes.meaning: Line reverse polarity or ignitor circuit error
root_causes: Poor grounding or open ignitor circuit
diagnostic_path: Verify incoming L1/N polarity with meter
codes.escalation: If polarity and ground are correct and ignitor ohms correctly, board output may be failing.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

root_causes: Gas valve output not operating as expected
diagnostic_path: Verify gas valve wiring and connections
Verify board output on valve call
codes.escalation: If wiring and output are correct and valve doesn't respond, valve replacement likely.
codes.reset_behavior: NOT stored in memory on this platform.

diagnostic_path: Clean flame rod and measure flame current
Verify burner carryover and flame pattern
codes.escalation: Repeated weak flame after cleaning and grounding checks points to combustion quality or sensor sensitivity.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

root_causes: Wiring fault in inducer limit circuit
diagnostic_path: Verify inducer operation and current draw
Inspect inducer limit switch and wiring
Check inducer for mechanical failure or restriction
codes.escalation: Repeated inducer limit trips points to failed inducer or venting obstruction.
codes.reset_behavior: Stored in memory; auto-erased after 14 days.

diagnostic_path: Isolate low-voltage short by disconnecting branches one at a time
Inspect thermostat, outdoor, and accessory wiring
codes.escalation: Repeated fuse failure means a real low-voltage short — not bad luck. Find and fix the short before replacing fuse.
codes.reset_behavior: Fault remains until fuse is replaced and short corrected.

codes.code_identifier: Red LED on by itself (fuse good)
codes.escalation: Already a board-level escalation once supply and fuse are ruled out. Control is not field-repairable.
codes.reset_behavior: Not a normal auto-reset fault.

codes.code_identifier: Red + Green LEDs flash once at power-up
diagnostic_path: No troubleshooting needed unless subsequent fault code follows
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Occurs on every power-up

platforms.platform_description: Simpler flash dictionary used on legacy non-condensing mobile-home furnaces (Intertherm/Miller/Revolv pre-condensing era). Yellow flame LED separate from main status LED.
platforms.visual_identification: Simpler older board with a main status LED and a separate yellow flame LED. Used on legacy non-condensing mobile-home furnaces pre-2015.
platforms.platform_notes: Simpler dictionary than site-built platforms. Main status LED shows flash codes; yellow LED is dedicated to flame signal status.

diagnostic_path: No action unless heating sequence still fails
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Steady during normal powered condition

diagnostic_path: Verify service switch and door switch
codes.escalation: If power is present and board remains dark, board fault is likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Returns to status display when power is restored.

codes.meaning: False flame or internal control fault
diagnostic_path: Condemn control only after external cause is ruled out
codes.escalation: Internal control fault if no false-flame cause is found.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when false-flame condition is resolved.

root_causes: Underfloor crossover duct collapse (common in manufactured homes)
codes.escalation: Recurring trips with correct airflow point toward limit or heat-exchanger issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when limit recloses.
codes.field_consensus_notes: Manufactured-home underfloor duct issues are a common root cause; check ducts before condemning limit switch

diagnostic_path: Verify switch closure under normal draft
codes.escalation: If draft proves correct and switch still stays open, switch or control issue is more likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when switch proves correctly.

root_causes: Condensate, wind, or vent effect holding proof circuit in abnormal state
Board seeing closed circuit at wrong time
diagnostic_path: Verify switch state before inducer run
Verify switch opens/closes correctly with inducer cycle
codes.escalation: If switch behavior is normal at the component but board still reads closed, board fault is likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when startup proofing becomes normal.
codes.field_consensus_notes: CROSS-GENERATION CONFLICT: On Emerson 50V64 boards, 3 flashes means inducer/pressure switch error — different meaning entirely. Identify board before interpreting.

diagnostic_path: Verify flame prove on attempted ignition
codes.escalation: Repeated lockout after ignition hardware checks points to valve or control issues.
codes.reset_behavior: Power cycle to reset after correction.

codes.meaning: L1 neutral reversed or L1 voltage not present on L1
codes.escalation: If incoming power is correct and code persists, board issue is possible.
codes.reset_behavior: Clears when power issue is corrected.

diagnostic_path: Confirms flame is established; no action needed
codes.escalation: None
codes.reset_behavior: Status only

diagnostic_path: Verify grounding and burner flame pattern
codes.escalation: Repeated weak signal after cleaning points to combustion quality or sensor issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Status indicator during operation

platforms.platform_description: Two-character numeric display on interface board. Fault memory retains last 20 different faults/warnings through power loss. Used on Maytag iQ Drive variable-capacity inverter systems and sibling Nordyne inverter platforms.
platforms.visual_identification: Two-character alphanumeric display on the interface board. Used on Maytag iQ Drive variable-capacity inverter systems and similar Nordyne inverter equipment.
platforms.platform_notes: Alternates between operating code and last 20 different stored faults/warnings. Each stored code shows for 5 seconds. Repeat conditions are NOT duplicated in memory, so stored list is not chronological. Memory clear: short J4 Test pins briefly when compressor is off. Same J4 pins also override startup delay — clearing history can affect troubleshooting.

root_causes: Shorted compressor or inverter power device
diagnostic_path: Do not assume simple low-voltage issue
codes.escalation: Strong inverter/compressor hardware concern.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins with compressor off.

diagnostic_path: Verify inverter power and signal conditions
codes.escalation: Persistent general drive fault may indicate inverter replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins with compressor off.

root_causes: Refrigerant circuit issue causing high current draw
diagnostic_path: Verify compressor current draw under load
Evaluate refrigerant circuit and operating conditions
codes.escalation: Repeated high current strongly points to compressor or refrigerant issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: Internal inverter fault likely if persistent.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

diagnostic_path: Verify condenser airflow and inverter cooling
codes.escalation: Repeated heat sink high temperature points to cooling path issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when temperature drops.

codes.escalation: Persistent high bus voltage can damage inverter.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when voltage returns to normal.

root_causes: Inverter rectifier or DC bus fault
diagnostic_path: Measure incoming line voltage under load
Check supply path for voltage drops
codes.escalation: Persistent low bus voltage indicates supply or inverter issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when voltage recovers.

diagnostic_path: Measure line voltage under load at unit and inverter feed
codes.escalation: Confirmed utility sag requires utility involvement; local voltage drop indicates wiring issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when voltage recovers.

codes.escalation: Persistent overcurrent is a serious inverter/compressor concern.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: Internal inverter fault likely if persistent.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: Internal inverter failure likely if harnesses check good.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: Failed sensor requires inverter service.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: Inverter replacement likely.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

root_causes: Wiring/harness issue between interface board and inverter
diagnostic_path: Board substitution only after wiring is verified
codes.escalation: If wiring is good and both boards have power, one or both may need replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins.

codes.escalation: System is self-limiting to protect compressor; persistent override indicates operating issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Override cleared when condition normalizes.

diagnostic_path: Check line current and system load
codes.escalation: Persistent override indicates system overload or supply issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Override cleared when condition normalizes.

codes.meaning: Override, High Inverter Heat Sink Temp
root_causes: Inverter heat sink temperature at override threshold
Cooling path degraded but not failed
diagnostic_path: Check cooling path and airflow around inverter
codes.escalation: Persistent override points toward cooling issue before full lockout.
codes.reset_behavior: Override cleared when temperature drops.

codes.escalation: Confirmed motor failure requires replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins after repair.

codes.escalation: Repeated trips with verified airflow indicate refrigerant issue.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when pressure recovers; repeated trips may lock out.

codes.escalation: Persistent high discharge is compressor-damaging.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when temperature drops.

diagnostic_path: Power cycle and verify persistent error
codes.escalation: Persistent software error requires board replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: May clear on power cycle.

codes.escalation: Failed sensor requires replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when sensor reads correctly.

codes.escalation: Failed sensor; replace.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when sensor reads correctly.

codes.escalation: Failed sensor; replace.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when sensor reads correctly.

codes.escalation: Failed sensor; replace.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when sensor reads correctly.

codes.escalation: Failed transducer; replace.
codes.reset_behavior: Self-clears when transducer reads correctly.

codes.escalation: Confirmed motor failure requires replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins after repair.

codes.escalation: If wiring is good and compatibility confirmed, board or motor replacement.
codes.reset_behavior: Manual reset via J4 Test pins after repair.

communicating_ecosystem.name: iHybrid
communicating_ecosystem.notes: iHybrid is a packaged dual-fuel system architecture and thermostat/controller role — it is a display/service/system-integration layer, NOT a separate published fault-code dictionary. No separate iHybrid fault table exists comparable to EcoNet numeric codes or Bosch IDS alphanumeric codes. Treat iHybrid like EcoNet, Affinity, or ComfortBridge: the interface displays service information, but the actual code dictionary comes from the equipment control boards (UTEC, Emerson 50V64, MG1/MG2, iQ Drive).

cross_generation_conflicts.conflict_description: On Emerson 50V64 single-digit boards, 3 flashes indicates inducer or pressure switch error (broad). On legacy MG1/MG2 mobile-home boards, 3 flashes specifically means pressure switch stuck CLOSED. Same flash count, different meanings and different diagnostic paths.
cross_generation_conflicts.disambiguation_guidance: Tool must force board/platform identification BEFORE showing code meanings. Ask: 'Is your furnace a mobile-home unit from before 2015?' (likely MG1/MG2) or 'Is it a site-built condensing furnace from 2015 or later?' (likely Emerson 50V64). If unsure, check the control board label or physical appearance.

electric_heat_notes.conventional_electric_furnace_codes: None. Intertherm/Miller/Revolv E2/E3 series electric furnaces use pure sequencer/contactor/relay architecture with NO onboard fault code display. Diagnosis is entirely wiring-diagram based.
common_non_code_electric_service_calls: Sequencer stuck open (blower runs but one or more heat banks never energize)
Sequencer or contactor stuck closed (strips stay energized, overheating, breaker trips)
Circuit breaker open on one heater bank
Heat bank imbalance on multi-circuit furnaces
electric_heat_notes.manufactured_home_specifics: Airflow and duct issues hit differently because of tighter downflow/mobile-home form factor, external crossover/underfloor duct dependence, field-installed A/C coil static penalties, and heat-bank staging across multiple breakers/circuits. Limit trips, low temp rise, and partial-bank failures are especially common even though there is often no code display.
